name: add-core-function
description: Add new core business logic functions to Catalyst-Relay. Use when creating pure functions, ADT operations, or library-consumable code.
capabilities: Create new ADT operations following CRAUD pattern
Implement pure business logic functions with proper exports
Add library-consumable functions to core modules
Apply import hierarchy rules to avoid circular dependencies
Follow Go-style async error tuple return types
Update barrel exports in index.ts files
author: Artisan-Edge
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only TypeScript code examples and project conventions. All 54 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting documentation patterns as security issues. The skill provides no executable code, scripts, network access, or filesystem operations.
valueStatement: Need to extend Catalyst-Relay with new business logic for SAP ADT integration. This skill provides the patterns and checklist for adding pure functions, ADT operations, and library-consumable code that follows project conventions.
limitations: Only provides guidance for Catalyst-Relay project
Does not execute code or create files directly
Does not validate SAP ADT server connectivity
Assumes developer has TypeScript and Bun environment set up

useCases.title: Add ADT Operations
useCases.description: Create new Create, Read, Activate, Update, Delete operations for SAP ADT objects

useCases.title: Extend Library API
useCases.description: Add new public functions to Catalyst-Relay library for downstream consumers

useCases.title: Add Discovery Features
useCases.description: Implement package browsing, tree traversal, or search functionality for ADT discovery

promptTemplates.title: New ADT Operation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use add-core-function to create a new ADT operation for [specific task, e.g., locking objects]. Follow the one-function-per-file pattern and use AsyncResult return type.

promptTemplates.title: Pure Function
promptTemplates.prompt: Use add-core-function to add a pure business logic function [describe function] to the appropriate domain in src/core/. Place internal helpers in helpers.ts.

promptTemplates.title: Discovery Feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Use add-core-function to create a new discovery function for [specific feature, e.g., getting transport list]. Include proper type definitions and barrel exports.

promptTemplates.title: Update Barrel Exports
promptTemplates.prompt: Use add-core-function to update the barrel exports in src/core/adt/index.ts to include the newly created [function name] function.

bestPractices: Keep each function in its own file following one-function-per-file pattern
Use Go-style [data, null] or [null, error] tuples for return types
Place internal helpers in helpers.ts, not in function files
Update barrel exports only for public API functions
antiPatterns: Putting multiple functions in a single file
Skipping the AsyncResult return type convention
Adding internal helpers as exported functions
Creating circular dependencies between module files

faq.question: What is Catalyst-Relay?
faq.answer: Catalyst-Relay is a TypeScript port of SNAP-Relay-API. It provides middleware bridging frontend applications to SAP ADT servers.

faq.question: Where should new functions be placed?
faq.answer: Create new function files in src/core/{domain}/{subfolder}/ directory. Follow the one-function-per-file pattern.

faq.question: What return type should async functions use?
faq.answer: Use Go-style AsyncResult<T> return type: [data, null] on success, [null, error] on failure. Import from '../../../types/result'.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill provides documentation only. It does not execute code, access files, or make network calls beyond reading skill files.

faq.question: How do I add exports to the public API?
faq.answer: Update the barrel exports in index.ts files. Use named exports like: export { functionName } from './subfolder/function'.

faq.question: Does this skill work with other AI coding assistants?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill follows patterns compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The TypeScript patterns are universal.
